name: Attio
one_liner: Attio is a flexible, AI-powered CRM platform designed for modern go-to-market teams, enabling them to manage customer relationships, automate workflows, and customize data models.
auth_notes: Attio supports OAuth 2.0 for applications serving multiple workspaces and API keys (Bearer tokens) for single-workspace integrations. HTTP Basic Authentication is also supported, though Bearer authentication is recommended. API keys are generated by workspace admins and are scoped. OAuth access tokens and single-workspace access tokens use scopes to control resource access and actions.
access_notes: For API keys, an admin of an Attio workspace can generate them from the 'Developers' tab in Workspace settings, available on all plans. For developer accounts and OAuth integrations (typically for publishing an integration or building for multiple workspaces), manual request via live chat or email to support@attio.com is required, with access typically granted within a couple of days. A free trial workspace can also serve as a developer sandbox.
api_notes: Attio provides a public REST API that exchanges JSON over HTTPS. It offers full CRUD (Create, Read, Update, Delete) access to core CRM entities such as records, lists, objects, attributes, tasks, notes, threads, comments, and webhooks. The API also supports custom fields and custom objects.
mcp_notes: Attio offers an official, hosted Model Context Protocol (MCP) server called 'Attio MCP'. This server allows AI tools like Claude, ChatGPT, and Cursor to securely interact with an Attio CRM workspace using OAuth authentication. There is also a community-built MCP server available on GitHub.

evidence.claim: Attio is a flexible, AI-powered CRM platform for modern go-to-market teams.
Attio supports OAuth 2.0, API keys (Bearer token), and HTTP Basic Authentication for its API.
API keys can be generated by an admin in Workspace settings, available on all plans. Developer accounts for OAuth require a manual request.
Attio provides a public REST API with full CRUD access to various CRM entities.
Attio has an official, hosted Model Context Protocol (MCP) server.
